title: Agent Step Record
description: Captures a single LLM agent node execution within a pipeline. Primary EU AI Act mapping: Art. 12(2), 12(3)(a)(b)(c), 14(4)(a).

record_id.description: Unique identifier for this record. Schema metadata; no direct Act mapping.

record_type.description: Discriminator identifying this record as an Agent Step Record.

pipeline_id.description: Identifier of the pipeline this record belongs to. EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(c) - supports operational monitoring (Art. 26(5)) by linking records to a pipeline run.

session_id.description: Identifier of the execution session within the pipeline. EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(c) - supports operational monitoring.

agent_id.description: Stable identifier of the agent node that produced this record. EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(c) - supports operational monitoring by identifying the agent.

model_id.description: Identifier of the LLM model used (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7'). EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(b) - enables post-market monitoring (Art. 72) and drift detection. Art. 14(4)(a) - enables oversight-time review of capabilities/limitations.

model_version.description: Specific version/snapshot of the model (e.g. '20260101', '1.2.3'). EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(b) - version granularity needed for drift detection. Art. 14(4)(a) - enables oversight-time review.

timestamp_start.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp marking the start of this agent step. EU AI Act Art. 12(3)(a) - record start time of each use (biometric minimum, applied universally per thesis policy).

timestamp_end.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp marking the end of this agent step. EU AI Act Art. 12(3)(a) - record end time of each use. Constraint timestamp_end >= timestamp_start is not expressible in JSON Schema 2020-12; enforced by the protocol validation layer at bundle seal time.

input_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of canonical input (prompt) given to the model. EU AI Act Art. 12(3)(c) - record input data that produced a result. Hashing rather than storing raw content satisfies GDPR data minimisation.

status.description: Outcome of the agent step. 'success' when the model returned a completion; 'error' when the call failed (timeout, provider error, exception). Required and explicit so a record is interpretable without knowing the protocol version. EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(a): recording events relevant to identifying situations in which the system may present a risk includes capturing malfunctions and failed steps, not only successful ones. On 'success' the output_hash field is present and the error field is absent; on 'error' output_hash is absent and error is present (value-comparison rule enforced at the validation layer, not in JSON Schema).

output_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of canonical model output. Present only when status is 'success' (a failed step has no output); absent otherwise. EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(c): supports operational monitoring by enabling integrity verification of agent output.

error.description: Structured failure detail. Present only when status is 'error'. EU AI Act Art. 12(2)(a): describes the malfunction that occurred.

reference_data_id.description: Identifier of any reference dataset/database consulted for this step (e.g. RAG corpus version). EU AI Act Art. 12(3)(b) - record the reference database consulted (biometric minimum, applied universally where relevant). Null when no external reference data is consulted.

parent_record_id.description: record_id of the immediately preceding record in the pipeline (inter-record linkage). Null for the first record. Schema metadata supporting the ordered record chain referenced by Art. 12(2) traceability.

runtime_metadata.description: Optional non-hashed forensic side channel; see the $defs/runtime_metadata definition. Not part of input_hash / output_hash, so those stay replay-stable, but covered by the Pipeline Bundle bundle_hash seal. No direct Act mapping; supports forensic correlation of a record with provider and framework logs.

uuid.description: RFC 4122 UUID string in lowercase hex (canonical form for the protocol).

sha256_hex.description: SHA-256 digest as 64 lowercase hex characters.

type.description: Class name of the error (e.g. 'TimeoutError', 'RateLimitError'). A low-PII identifier suitable for an audit trail.

message_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical error message. Mirrors the input_hash/output_hash privacy stance (GDPR data minimisation); null when no message detail is available.

source.description: Component whose invocation raised the error (the failure boundary, not a deep root-cause classification). The reference LangChain adapter emits 'provider' (LLM call) and 'tool' (tool call); 'network', 'middleware', and 'unknown' are reserved for other adapters or deployments.

retryable.description: Whether the failure is considered transient and safe to retry, when the deployment can determine this.

error_detail.description: Structured failure detail attached to a record whose status is 'error'.

runtime_metadata.description: Optional forensic side channel carrying the runtime identifiers that the semantic projection removes from input_hash / output_hash. It is deliberately excluded from those content commitments so they stay replay-stable across identical runs, but it is covered by the Pipeline Bundle bundle_hash seal, so its values remain tamper-evident. Its purpose is to let an investigator cross-reference a record with the LLM provider's request log or the agent framework's execution trace. additionalProperties is left open because the identifiers available are adapter- and framework-specific; the properties below are the keys the reference LangChain adapter populates.

run_id.description: Identifier of the framework execution (the LangChain callback run id) that produced this record. Correlates the record with the agent framework's own execution trace.

message_id.description: Runtime identifier the provider stamped on the response message (e.g. an AIMessage id). Correlates the record with the provider's request log. Present when the response carried an id; the semantic projection keeps this identifier out of output_hash.

tool_call_ids.description: Maps each canonical tool-call label ('0', '1', '2', ...) that the projection writes into output_hash to the real runtime tool_call_id it replaced. Lets a forensic investigator resolve a hashed canonical label back to the provider-assigned tool_call_id.
